CPX Main Menu Commands

The main menu that appears at the top of the CPX window has the following options:

  • File
  • Edit
  • View
  • Help

File

The File option has the following commands:

New Window
Launches a new instance of CPX. The shortcut key for this menu item is CTRL+N.
Open
Opens an existing CPX (.cpx) data file. The shortcut key for this menu item is CTRL+O.
Save As

Saves the collected data in the current CPX session under a different file name. The shortcut key for this menu item is CTRL+S.

Note    

CPX Does Not Allow Saving to Original File if Symbol Information Was Incorrect.

If you save CPX performance data to a file without symbol information, then later attempt to resave the data with symbol information, CPX will not allow you to save to the same file. Instead, CPX requires that you use the Save As menu item to save to a different filename.

Saving CPX data without symbol information can occur if the symbols path is not set correctly or the PDB file is missing.

Record
Launches the Record Dialog Box which can be used to start a new profiling session. This command can also be started by clicking on the main toolbar.
Export Data to .CSV
Exports the selected data in the current session to an Excel .csv file. If no data is selected, the entire data set is exported.
Recent Files
Displays a list of recently accesssed .cpx files.
Exit
Closes CPX.

Edit

The Edit option has the following commands:

Copy
Copies the current selection to the clipboard. The shortcut key for this menu item is CTRL+C.
Select All
Selects all items in the active window. The shortcut key for this menu item is CTRL+A.
Find
Finds the line containing the specified text. The shortcut key for this menu item is CTRL+F.
Find Next
Find the next instance of a line containing the text previously specified by Find. The shortcut key for this menu item is F3.
Go To Line
Jumps to the specified line in the Source File View. This option is available only when the Source File view is active and a source file has been loaded. The shortcut key for this menu option is CTRL+G.

View

The View option has the following commands:

Toolbar
Toggles the main toolbar.
Status Bar
Toggles the status bar.
TopX
Shows the profile data in a flat list sorted according to the user. For more details, see CPX TopX View. The shortcut key for this menu item is CTRL+ALT+X.
Call Stack
Shows the profile data in a hierarchical manner, sorted using the call stack data. For more details, see CPX Call Stack View. The shortcut key for this menu item is CTRL+ALT+C.
File Info
Displays information about the profiling session that created the file. For more details, see CPX File Info View. The shortcut key for this menu item is CTRL+ALT+F.
Source File
Shows source code for a function clicked upon in one of the other views. For more details, see CPX Source File View. The shortcut key for this menu item is CTRL+ALT+O.
Butterfly
Toggles the Butterfly view. This view is useful for determining the caller and callee of a given function. For more details, see CPX Butterfly View. The shortcut key for this menu item is CTRL+ALT+B. This command can also be started by clicking on the main toolbar.
Settings
Launches the Settings dialog box which is used to set symbol paths, the event sampled, and the frequency of the sampling.

Help

The Help option has the following commands:

Help with CPU Profiler for Xbox
Launches the online help for CPX. The shortcut key for this menu option is F1.
About CPU Profiler for Xbox
Displays the CPX version and copyright information.
